data.body:
Evaluated and realigned the agentic code-review epic dowdy-swear-bird (b744-4fb9-2d05-4b49) against the current codebase, then decomposed and certified it.

=== FINDINGS / REALIGNMENT ===
- The epic was written against a SUPERSEDED three-pass framework (9da1/sheer-myth-idol). That was replaced by 3316/vivid-gang-day — the FOUR-PASS review KERNEL (Pass-1 finder evidence; Pass-2 separate binary verifier; Pass-3 deterministic decide; Pass-4 coach), CLOSED and shipped as src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (verify.py/decide.py/coach.py, 749 LOC). docs/review-kernel.md names b744 as 'the second consumer'.
- So b744 is now a kernel CONSUMER (supply diff-assembler + finder/verify/coach prompts + code move-catalog; call verify_findings/pass3_over_findings/coach), NOT a from-scratch passes build. code_review.py is a throwaway single-pass demo to RETIRE.
- Verified the kernel is gate-agnostic (smoke-tested pass3_decide/coach/validate_move_registry with a CODE move-catalog).
- The overlay 'agent-triggers-an-overlay' requirement = the APPROVED base->overlay escalation design recorded as a comment on 9da1: base reviewer emits enum-constrained recommend_overlays; deterministic overlay_union = (glob-triggers UNION base.recommend) - already-run, ONE-HOP, capped; all findings flow into the SAME Pass-2/3.

=== DERISKING EXPERIMENTS (real v3 engine, offline, no tokens) ===
- Built a runnable two-round overlay-escalation workflow: triggers(overlay_triggers) -> base(agent) -> round_a(batch when:=triggers) -> overlay_union(uses) -> round_b(batch when:=union) -> merge -> gate. PROVED: the base agent's recommend_overlays drove Round-B membership; bogus overlay ids dropped by the enum; the per-change cap bounded fan-out; one-hop held; findings merged and reached the deterministic gate.
- CONCLUSION: NO core workflow-engine change needed. batch membership is a static authored list gated per-item by `when: ${{ steps.union.outputs.include_<overlay> }}` (same pattern plan-review uses). Only NEW engine-side code = two trivial scripted ops (overlay_union, merge_findings). map exists but does not reduce outputs, so batch is the right construct.
- Prior-art grounding: separate-verifier + deterministic severity (SAFE/Wei 2024; 'LLMs cannot self-correct' Huang 2023); router->specialist capped one-hop dispatch (Anthropic multi-agent system; RouteLLM); scanner-as-ground-truth + LLM-as-judge (Semgrep 96%; arxiv 2510.10290; GitHub secret-scanning fail-closed).

=== ACTIONS TAKEN ===
- Updated worktree from origin/main (ff to 007ee59ce).
- Rewrote b744 description + ACs onto the 4-pass kernel; partitioned ACs into (A) Capability [unblocked now] and (B) Dogfood/Gerrit [gated on d251]; added dependency analysis + a 'why not split' decision (partition instead).
- Dependency verdict: kernel(3316), runner+engine(e6d9), reviewer prompts(da27), grounding registry(8f6c) ALL shipped/closed. Only LIVE external blocker is d251-05e1-eb55-43b2 (AWS Gerrit provisioning), and only for the dogfood/voter ACs. 9da1 blocker link is stale (superseded by 3316).
- Decomposed into 6 child stories (deps captured as WS-aligned epic ACs), each with file_impact + verify_commands, sequenced via depends_on:
  WS1 8e6f-3ad2-1f24-4b8b low-cloud-wreck (diff assembler + base reviewer) — READY
  WS2 8e78-d515-3a98-4eba make-rant-buoy (overlay catalog + prompts + move-catalog) dep WS1
  WS3 8855-e5f9-05b4-4a04 crow-knelt-fiber (overlay_union + merge_findings + code-review.yaml) dep WS2
  WS4 98ff-d3b8-d2f7-4a86 side-haiku-dew (gate_dispatch + source-separation) dep WS3
  WS5 bba6-69b9-b9d4-4ac0 corn-pub-oat (secrets/security detectors, fail-closed) dep WS3
  WS6 4119-1a4f-19ff-4cfa virid-trench-stone (Gerrit voter + dogfood) dep WS4; gated on d251

=== CERTIFICATION ===
- Ran the four-pass plan-review (local build, --source local --no-sign) on the epic + all 6 children. ALL SEVEN VERDICT=PASS, zero blocking findings, no Layer-1 floor failures. Advisories all minor/none (non-blocking). Store validate: no critical/major introduced; sibling depends_on landed at story level (no mis-promotion).
- Remediated substantive advisories across epic+children (overlay-id enum ownership; CI-drift-gate defined as concrete observable; threshold defaults 0.95/advisory-by-default; typed code_review_verdict schema + REVIEW_RESULT sidecar; fail-closed=BLOCK; Gerrit label/auth/copyCondition/vote-mapping deferred to d251; back-out plan for the destructive single-pass retirement). Post-remediation re-run re-confirmed PASS; residual advisories are non-blocking AC-phrasing nitpicks left intentionally.

=== STATE ===
b744 OPEN with 6 children (WS1-WS6); WS1-WS5 unblocked, WS6 blocked by d251. Two provenance comments on b744. No code changes made (analysis + ticket work only). Experiment scripts in the job tmp dir (overlay_escalation_experiment.py) could be committed as offline regression coverage if desired.

NOTE: rebar config emits 'unknown key verify.require_plan_review_for_claim ignored' even on the local build — worth investigating separately (the plan-review CLAIM gate may not be enforced).
